Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/process_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity in strict/hardened modes, and fix any matrix-path regressions the new coverage exposes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:52:12.955090641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:52:12.955090641Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3jzb","title":"BRAVO RW Locks for read-heavy paths (section 14.7)","description":"Implement BRAVO (Biased Reader And Visible Observer) reader-writer locks for read-heavy paths in FrankenLibC. BRAVO is a RW lock optimized for the common case of read-dominant workloads. Readers use a visible-reader protocol (write their TID to a shared slot on read entry, clear on exit). Writers check all slots for active readers. Key innovation: biased locking — the fast path for readers is a single atomic write (no CAS, no fence on x86-TSO). Writers pay the cost of scanning reader slots. Application to FrankenLibC: (1) Allocation metadata — the per-arena metadata (free list head, allocation count, generation counters) is read on every allocation for validation but written infrequently (only on actual alloc/free state changes). BRAVO eliminates reader contention. (2) TSM certificate cache — evaluated certificates are cached. Cache reads vastly outnumber writes (cache invalidation is rare). (3) Thread registry — the list of active threads is read by garbage collection and RCU grace period detection but modified only on thread creation/destruction. Performance target: reader fast path <5ns (single atomic store), writer path <100ns (slot scan + fence). Comparison: implement alongside RCU (bd-3aof) and Left-Right (bd-cs9w) — benchmark all three for FrankenLibC's specific read/write ratios to determine which is optimal for each use case.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.7 of graveyard. BRAVO from Dice, Kogan, & Herlihy (2019 USENIX ATC). Visible-reader protocol for biased RW locks optimized for read-dominant workloads.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- BravoRwLock<T>: wraps T with visible-reader protocol.
- Reader fast path: atomic store of TID to slot in reader table. Single store on x86-TSO (no fence needed).
- Reader table: #[repr(align(64))] array of AtomicU64 slots, sized to MAX_THREADS.
- Writer: acquire inner lock (Mutex), scan reader table for active readers, wait for all to clear.
- Slot assignment: hash(thread_id) mod TABLE_SIZE for O(1) slot lookup. Collision handling: linear probe.
- read() -> BravoReadGuard: write TID to slot, read data, clear slot on drop.
- write() -> BravoWriteGuard: acquire mutex, scan table, return mutable reference.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. BravoRwLock<T> compiles and passes miri for basic read/write cycle.
2. Reader fast path <5ns at p99 (single atomic store on x86-TSO).
3. Writer path <100ns at p99 including slot scan under 16-reader load.
4. 32-reader + 1-writer stress test: 1M operations, TSan clean.
5. Compare with RCU and Left-Right: benchmark all three on FrankenLibC metadata workload.
6. No reader starvation: readers always make progress regardless of writer activity.
7. Slot collision rate <5% at 64 threads (hash quality test).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: bravo, operation=read, slot, reader_count) per read (disabled in release).
- tracing::debug!(target: bravo, operation=write, scan_time_ns, active_readers_found) per write.
- Benchmark comparison: bravo_vs_rcu_vs_leftright.json with throughput and latency per read/write ratio.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:46.141109287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:14.456054507Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:14.455840361Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jzb","depends_on_id":"bd-3aof","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kh","title":"Kernel: Proof-carrying policy tables (integrate into decide/repair selection)","description":"Wire policy table into runtime decision.

Rules:
- Strict mode: table should not change externally visible semantics; only controls validation depth.
- Hardened mode: table may choose Repair vs Deny vs FullValidate, but must respect barrier constraints.

Outputs:
- Snapshot exports policy hash and selected action distribution.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:33:33.862452216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:04:10.442423867Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:50:19.509740823Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kh","depends_on_id":"bd-20s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3kh","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":998,"issue_id":"bd-3kh","author":"FuchsiaHollow","text":"Integrated policy table into decide() with policy_escalation pattern. PolicyTableLookup struct provides O(1) lookup from owned bytes. Conservative merge: table can only escalate profile, never de-escalate. Action cascade order invariant preserved by extracting Deny/Repair/FullValidate into policy_escalation variable (Allow defers to normal cascade). Snapshot exports policy_hash_prefix and action distribution. All 788 membrane tests pass, full workspace clean.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:49:58Z"},{"id":999,"issue_id":"bd-3kh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: Fixed strict-mode Deny bug - PCPT Deny/Repair clamp to FullValidate in strict mode. Added 6 integration tests. All 794 membrane tests pass. Golden snapshot and perf baseline updated.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:04:10Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ku","title":"RaptorQ Runtime: Integrate evidence encoding with runtime_math + commitment audit","description":"Wire evidence capture into existing runtime telemetry.

Plan:
- On observe_validation_result(): append systematic evidence records cheaply.
- On cadence (e.g., every 256 obs): generate repair symbols and append.
- Feed commitment_audit with hashes of evidence segments to make tamper-evident.

Acceptance criteria:
- Evidence capture is bounded and does not allocate on hot path.
- Snapshot exports evidence health (loss rate, overhead_percent, last epoch id).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:56.262792501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:40:54.654618851Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:40:43.345912194Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ku","depends_on_id":"bd-1es","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ku","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ku","depends_on_id":"bd-kom","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1000,"issue_id":"bd-3ku","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DONE. Evidence recording integrated into decide(). Box SystematicEvidenceLog 512 field, cadence-gated at 16384 calls plus all adverse decisions. Snapshot exports evidence_seqno/loss_count/max_epoch. 3 integration tests, all 812 membrane tests pass, all quality gates green.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:40:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kz","title":"Kernel: Alpha-Investing FDR (integrate into RuntimeMathKernel)","description":"Wire alpha-investing into the runtime decision law.

Integration plan:
- decide(): use alpha budget to gate escalation to FullValidate/Repair when multiple monitors are firing.
- observe_validation_result(): feed outcomes back into alpha-wealth updates.
- snapshot(): export wealth/spend/rejections + any per-family state.
- fusion: add severity signal for alpha state if needed.

Acceptance criteria:
- Conservative merge: alpha-investing may ONLY escalate or throttle within documented safe bounds (no unsafe de-escalation under high risk).
- Snapshot schema updated additively.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:08.263284738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:25:38.272517946Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:25:38.272481989Z","close_reason":"Alpha-Investing fully integrated into RuntimeMathKernel: wired in observe_validation_result() with base_severity input, state cached to atomic, fusion signal[59], 3 snapshot fields (wealth/rejections/fdr). Commit debf416. Force-closing: controller registration guidelines (bd-2vf) now closed too.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kz","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3kz","depends_on_id":"bd-9co","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1001,"issue_id":"bd-3kz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"VERIFIED by GentleOwl: Full integration in RuntimeMathKernel confirmed (commit debf416). Ready to close once bd-9co and bd-2vf resolve.","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:20:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ld","title":"Kernel: Groebner normal form (tests + perf)","description":"Prove canonicalization stability and cost.

Tests:
- Equivalent signatures reduce to same normal form.
- Reduction is idempotent.
- Bounded runtime.

Perf:
- Ensure reducer is O(1) with small table; bench overhead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderStone","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:55.255732792Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:09.351554975Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:00.707998302Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ld","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ld","depends_on_id":"bd-380","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ld","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1002,"issue_id":"bd-3ld","author":"CobaltCompass","text":"Verified complete by CobaltCompass. Tests in grobner.rs cover: (1) equivalent_signatures_reduce_to_same_normal_form, (2) reduction_is_idempotent (exhaustive 2^6=64 patterns), (3) confluence_all_64_support_patterns (convergence proof), (4) bounded_runtime_canonical_classification (100k ops < 200ms), (5) reducer_is_o1_with_canonical_table (max steps <= 3), (6) perf_no_degradation_at_large_masks (high-bit independence), (7) canonical_class_covers_all_ids (full coverage). All acceptance criteria met.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:09Z"}]}
